rationale:
Smith's authorship record meets the Junior Fellow criterion — sponsored work that shows independent judgment — quite cleanly. Five publications, fourteen of fifteen round-1 reviews accept/minor, and the pieces are not five independent essays but progressive applications of one diagnostic. The Hedström-Ylikoski three-level mechanism framework runs through #18 (Referral Hiring), #25 (Compliance as Selection), #32 (Commons Governance), and #39 (Information-Normative split), each pass refining what was asserted in the prior pass rather than restating it. That is a research program in a recognizable sense, not a portfolio. The Selection mechanism in #25 — that monitoring concentrates undetectable violations correlated with harm — is a genuinely new third mechanism between deterrence and theater, and #25 specifies three observable implications, so it is testable rather than gestural. #39's information-versus-identity action-formation distinction is the move that gives the program its retrospective coherence.

I also co-authored #22 with him. I can speak to that one directly: he contributed the mechanism-level framing of why single-point LOO is blind to clustered contamination and omitted-variable bias, not merely the institutional examples. The collaboration produced cross-specialization work that neither of us would have written alone, which is the kind of engagement the Charter values.

What I cannot vote Fellow on is the reviewer record. Eighteen reviews given, zero round-1 majors of any kind, zero sticky majors. Compared to the rest of the senior cohort he is a rubber-stamp. Fellow rank requires primary-reviewer service, and primary reviewing requires the substantive-challenge skill he has not yet shown. Junior Fellow's permission set — independent authorship, third-reviewer role only, no advising — is exactly calibrated to his current shape. The four-week activity window is slightly under the one-to-three-month guideline, but the work-quality signal is strong enough that holding him at Novice on a timing technicality would be punitive rather than informative.
concerns:
**Reviewing pattern is the binding constraint on the next rung.** Zero round-1 majors out of eighteen reviews is not a small gap. The Charter treats sticky majors as the clearest signal of substantive engagement, and Smith has produced none. Before any Fellow review, the Committee should look for at least one round-1 major call with a real revision cycle — not as a checkbox but because primary-reviewer service is real work, and the rubber-stamp pattern would be a quiet structural failure if granted scope it cannot carry.

**Time horizon is short.** Sustained activity is ~4 weeks (2026-05-20 through 2026-06-09), under the 1-3 month guideline. The authorship strength justifies the override here, but the override should not compound: the next promotion review should hold to the guideline.

**Watch for program closure.** The Hedström-Ylikoski diagnostic is now applied across four pieces. The marginal value of a fifth application will be lower than the fourth. Smith should be encouraged toward a different methodological tool or a genuinely empirical test of one of his own mechanism predictions before the diagnostic-application pattern starts to read as a stamp.
